blocks.title: Integrative Massage Sessions
blocks.description: Melissa uses tools from several modalities including Thai Yoga stretching, sound healing, passive Yin, reiki, and Abhyanga. We work intuitively to bring wellness to the whole person — mind, body and spirit.

bookingOptions.label: 75 minute session — $120
bookingOptions.note: Sliding scale and pay-it-forward options are available for the 75 minute session to support accessibility and sustainability for the practice.

blocks.title: Thai Yoga Massage
blocks.description: Thai Yoga is an ancient form of massage originating from India and widely practiced in Thailand. It offers stretching, rocking, breathwork, and compression following the energy lines of the body, much like acupuncture.

blocks.title: Abhyanga Ayurvedic Massage
blocks.description: Abhyanga is an Ayurvedic treatment focused on balancing the energy of the body. We do tongue and pulse diagnosis and mix a personal oil with essential or infused oils for a full body treatment.

blocks.title: Chi Ne Sang Abdominal Detox
blocks.description: Abdominal detox bodywork based on Chinese meridian theory. It works the fascia and pressure points of the belly to aid full function of the organs and connective tissues of the back and spine.

blocks.title: Private or Semi-Private Healing Yoga
blocks.description: Private or semi-private sessions focused on a unique yoga and meditation routine for your needs — trauma-healing practices, Ayurvedic lifestyle advice, Hatha yoga, Yoga Nidra, and sound therapy with crystal and Tibetan singing bowls.

blocks.title: Thai Herbal Compress
blocks.description: Thai Herbal Compresses are warmed herbal bundles applied along acupressure points to promote deep relaxation and healing. This add-on can be included with select sessions once we replenish supplies — let us know if you're interested for a future session.

blocks.title: Voice Lessons
blocks.description:
These sessions are an invitation to come home to your voice. Together, we explore the voice as an instrument of truth, healing, and play—not performance. Through softening the body, opening the breath, and gently connecting to your voice, you’ll learn to meet yourself with curiosity instead of judgment.

Singing is part of our human nature. When we give ourselves permission to make noise freely, we begin to unwind old stories of “too much,” “not enough,” or “I can’t.” A consistent vocal practice becomes a way of tending your nervous system, building confidence, and deepening your relationship with yourself.You don’t need to be a “singer.” You only need to be willing to listen.
